curriculum_alignment.subject: mathematics
curriculum_alignment.seeds_of_change: Investigate linear relationships on a coordinate grid, connect patterns in x and y values to the form y = mx + c, and use gradient and intercept to model and interpret straight-line situations. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

verse_ality.driving_question: How can straight-line graphs help us predict, design, and explain relationships in the real world?

knowing_world.focus: Straight lines show a consistent relationship between x and y values that can be represented by an equation in the form y = mx + c, where m is the gradient and c is the y-intercept.
key_concepts: Equation of a straight line (y = mx + c)
tools: Interactive sketch: explore how changing points changes the line and its equation

human_story.focus: Linear patterns appear in daily life (budgeting, prediction, distance-time) and connect to the historical development of algebra and analytic geometry through thinkers such as Euclid and Diophantus.

expressive_self.focus: Create and communicate your own linear relationships through drawing, listing points, forming rules, and expressing what “linear” means through metaphor or short poetic writing.
activities: Draw your own line, list five points on it, and write a rule connecting x and y.
Create a metaphor or short poem for a linear relationship and compare it to a non-linear one.

designed_world.focus: Use linear equations as design tools in construction, finance, physics and coding; investigate how engineers and systems use linear models to plan paths, distances, and infrastructure.
design_challenges: Use the interactive sketch to record coordinate changes and write the exact equation of the line.

regulation_supports: Breathing and tracing a steady left-to-right line motion to connect linearity with steadiness and predictability.
wellbeing_prompts: How does the steadiness of a linear pattern feel in your body?

pathways.name: Final Task - SDG 9 Bridge Line Model
pathways.focus: Model a straight bridge as a line through two given points and find the equation of that line.
products: A plotted coordinate diagram showing the points (2, 3) and (8, 7)
A correct linear equation for the bridge
A short reflection on how mathematics supports innovation in infrastructure
assessment_criteria: Conceptual Understanding: Explains how gradient and intercept relate to a straight line (y = mx + c)
Fluency & Accuracy: Correct gradient calculation and correct final equation
Application: Clear link to the bridge/infrastructure context (SDG 9)
Communication & Reflection: Reasoning is clear and reflection is thoughtful

required: Plot coordinates on a Cartesian grid
Work with negative and positive numbers
Understand basic substitution into an expression
Find gradient as change in y over change in x (rise/run)

builds_toward: Graphing and interpreting y = mx + c
